name: Dragon's Pearl
description: The Dragon's Pearl, also known as the Dragon Ball or Dragon Orb, is a potent symbol of spiritual energy, wisdom, prosperity, and power in Chinese mythology and culture. It is frequently depicted as a luminous sphere held by Chinese dragons, often under their chin or clutched in their claws. The pearl represents the dragon's essence and the culmination of its life force. Its origins are deeply intertwined with the dragon's role as a benevolent and powerful creature associated with water, weather, and good fortune. Possessing the Dragon's Pearl is believed to grant immense power, control over the elements (particularly water and weather), and the ability to communicate with dragons. It can bring prosperity, good health, and spiritual enlightenment to its holder. The Dragon's Pearl is not merely a physical object but a conduit for divine energy and a symbol of the interconnectedness between the mortal and spiritual realms. Its significance permeates Chinese art, literature, and folklore, representing the ultimate attainment of wisdom and the embodiment of auspicious power. Legends say that only those with pure hearts and noble intentions can truly harness the pearl's full potential, lest its power corrupt them. The pearl's luminescence symbolizes clarity of thought and the dispelling of ignorance. It is a treasure sought after by emperors, sages, and those seeking to elevate themselves beyond the mundane.
subtitle: Source of Dragon's Power and Wisdom
origin: Born from the breath or life essence of a dragon.
currentLocation: Varied locations; often hidden in the depths of oceans or mountains.

keyMyths.title: The Dragon's Gift
keyMyths.description: A benevolent dragon gifts a pearl to a virtuous farmer, bringing prosperity to his village and ending a long drought. The pearl symbolizes the dragon's gratitude and the farmer's pure heart.

keyMyths.title: The Stolen Pearl
keyMyths.description: A greedy emperor attempts to steal a dragon's pearl, but is met with the dragon's wrath and learns a valuable lesson about respect and balance. This story highlights the consequences of greed and the importance of harmony with nature.

sources.author: E.T.C. Werner
sources.description: A comprehensive collection of Chinese myths and legends.
sources.text: A comprehensive collection of Chinese myths and legends.
